actionable_predicate: Before stepping away from a hard engineering moment, identify which specific peers will witness the withdrawal, and what their default-model of mid-execution exit looks like. The shape of the exit (handover quality, public-record explanation, runbook completeness) is the only input that reroutes that model.
roadmap_application: PM use: in a senior-engineer transition conversation, ask the structured question — 'Who in your peer-network will see this withdrawal? What is their default-explanation? What shape of exit changes their default?' This gives a structured way to plan transitions that protect the engineer's reputation while honoring the duty.
verdict_reason: The implication is testable. Falsifiability narrows the claim to a specific class of peer-cognition events (not all reputation concerns matter equally). Counter_example handles the compromised-peer-network case. PASS on both 7.3 and 3.11.
